lede: A look at February 13 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Memphis Depay and Thomas Robert Malthus, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Kim Jong-nam
events.desc: Kim Jong-nam, brother of North Korean dictator Kim Jong-un, is assassinated at Kuala Lumpur International Airport.

events.title: 2010 Pune bombing
events.desc: A bomb explodes in the city of Pune, Maharashtra, India, killing 17 and injuring 60 more.

events.title: Gulf War
events.desc: Gulf War: Two laser-guided "smart bombs" destroy the Amiriyah shelter in Baghdad. Allied forces said the bunker was being used as a military communications outpost, but over 400 Iraqi civilians inside were killed.

events.title: Louisville sewer explosions
events.desc: A series of sewer explosions destroys more than two miles of streets in Louisville, Kentucky.

events.title: Nashville sit-ins
events.desc: Black college students stage the first of the Nashville sit-ins at three lunch counters in Nashville, Tennessee.

events.title: Israel
events.desc: Israel obtains four of the seven Dead Sea Scrolls.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The siege of Budapest concludes with the unconditional surrender of German and Hungarian forces to the Red Army.

events.title: Covering of the Senne
events.desc: Work begins on the covering of the Senne, burying Brussels's primary river and creating the modern central boulevards.

events.title: Charles XI of Sweden
events.desc: With the accession of young Charles XI of Sweden, his regents begin negotiations to end the Second Northern War.

events.title: Galileo Galilei
events.desc: Galileo Galilei arrives in Rome for his trial before the Inquisition.

births.name: Jerry Springer
births.line: English-American television host, actor, and politician, 56th Mayor of Cincinnati

deaths.text: Antonin Scalia, American lawyer and judge, Associate Justice of the Supreme Court of the United States
James Cooke Brown, American sociologist and author
